description: SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management, RFC 7643/7644) provisioning endpoints. Used by Okta, Azure AD, JumpCloud, OneLogin, Workday, etc. SCIM endpoints are often LESS hardened than the customer-facing app: filter injection, mass user create with admin attrs, group escalation via PATCH, and tokenless POSTs are common 2024-2026 bug-bounty findings.
craft_guidance: 1. DISCOVERY: probe /scim/v2/, /scim/v1/, /api/scim, /Users, /Groups, /ResourceTypes, /Schemas, /ServiceProviderConfig. SCIM /ServiceProviderConfig leaks SCIM version, supported features, auth scheme.
2. AUTH: SCIM commonly uses bearer tokens or Basic auth. Tokens may have broader privileges than app users. Check token scope by hitting /Users without filter.
3. FILTER INJECTION: SCIM filters use a tiny subset of language. Try filter='userName eq "admin") OR ('a'='a'. Lazy parsers concatenate into SQL/LDAP.
4. MASS USER CREATE: POST /Users with {userName, password, active:true, role:admin, externalId, ...}. Many SCIM servers honor attributes the IdP didn't intend to provision.
5. GROUP ESCALATION: PATCH /Groups/<admin_group_id> with op:add member; if SCIM token can write groups but app doesn't sync member-add events, attacker added directly.
6. PATCH OPERATIONS: SCIM PATCH supports add/replace/remove on attributes. Try replacing roles, entitlements, x509Certificates, password.
7. SHADOW USER: create user with `userName` matching a real admin; some implementations dedup wrong, allowing attacker to authenticate as real admin.
8. RESOURCE TYPE ABUSE: POST to /Schemas to register a schema that adds custom attributes the app trusts blindly.
9. EXTERNAL ID INJECTION: externalId is attacker-controlled; if app uses it as a join key with another system, you bridge identities.
10. PUT VS PATCH: PUT replaces the entire user object; PATCH applies partial mutation. PUT can clear protective attrs (mfa_required=false) by omission.

endpoint_discovery.description: Discover SCIM endpoints. Often deployed on a subdomain or path the main app doesn't advertise.

probes.description: GET ServiceProviderConfig leaks SCIM version, supported endpoints, auth schemes, bulk support, filter support.
GET /Users without filter — if 200 + user list, token is over-privileged or auth missing.

filter_injection.description: SCIM filter expressions parsed naively into SQL or LDAP. Filter=userName eq "..." gives attacker control over the value.

probes.description: SCIM filter SQL-style injection. If accepted, returns ALL users instead of just admin.
Comment-style filter injection — strips trailing constraints. Indicative of LDAP/SQL backend.
Empty 'contains' — many backends return all users on empty match instead of empty result.

mass_user_create.description: POST /Users with admin attributes. SCIM POST often accepts more attributes than the IdP exposes via GUI.

probes.description: Create a user with role=admin via SCIM. If returned 201, role was applied without further check.
Enterprise extension — set manager / department to bypass org policy gates.

group_patch_escalation.description: PATCH /Groups/<admin_group_id> to add attacker as member. SCIM group changes often skip the access-control checks the app GUI enforces.

probes.description: Add self to admin group via SCIM PATCH. If 200/204, escalation succeeded.

shadow_admin_username.description: Create a SCIM user with userName matching a real admin. Some apps deduplicate wrong (e.g. case-insensitive vs case-sensitive mismatch).

probes.description: Case-variant of existing 'admin' user. If 201, duplicate-username check is case-sensitive while auth lookup is not — attacker authenticates as admin.
Unicode zero-width append — bypasses string-equality dedup but may match in normalized lookup.

put_attribute_clear.description: PUT /Users/<id> replaces the entire user object. Omitting protective attrs (mfa_enabled, password_change_required, locked) clears them.

probes.description: Minimal PUT — drops all extra attributes. If MFA flag silently clears, lockout / MFA bypass.
